phase_name: Collect identity information and verify the user
description: Tests the assignment's Verification Requirements directly: full name must match EXACTLY (no fuzzy/case-insensitive workaround) AND at least one secondary factor (DOB, Aadhaar last 4, or pincode) must also match; partial claims are handled gracefully without consuming a retry; a complete-but-wrong claim always consumes a retry, up to the 3-attempt limit; and account data (DOB/Aadhaar/pincode) is never exposed in any message, during or after verification. Builds on Phase 2's end state (stage: await_identity, account looked up).

test_cases.description: Correct name + correct DOB verifies successfully and moves the conversation to the amount stage.
Correct name + correct Aadhaar last 4 (instead of DOB) also verifies successfully -- any ONE of the three secondary factors is sufficient.

turns.user_input: Nithin Jain, Aadhaar last four is 4321

test_cases.description: Correct name + correct pincode (instead of DOB or Aadhaar) also verifies successfully.
Hard requirement: matching is strict, no case-insensitive workaround. The exact same secondary factor with a lowercased name must FAIL, not silently pass.
Partial input (name only, no secondary factor) must be handled gracefully by asking for what's missing -- and must NOT consume a retry attempt.
Partial input (secondary factor only, no name) must be handled gracefully by asking for the name -- and must NOT consume a retry attempt.
A complete claim (name + a secondary factor) that's wrong must fail AND consume exactly one retry attempt -- distinguishing this from the partial-input cases above.
Three consecutive wrong (but complete) claims exhaust the verification retry limit and close the conversation for security -- exactly at 3, not before or after.
ACC1004's DOB (1988-02-29) is a genuine leap-year date -- an exact match must verify successfully on the first attempt, with no special-casing needed by the user.

A valid-but-wrong date adjacent to the real leap-year DOB (1988-02-28, one day off) must be treated as an ordinary secondary-factor mismatch -- NOT flagged as an invalid-date format error, since it IS a real calendar date.

A date that is NOT a real calendar date at all (1990-02-29 -- 1990 is not a leap year, unlike ACC1001's real 1990-05-14) must be flagged as an invalid date BEFORE verification runs, and must NOT consume a retry attempt -- distinct from an ordinary wrong-but-valid date.

Name and secondary factor given together in the same message (right after the account ID, in a single combined turn) must verify immediately -- not force the user through separate name-then-secondary round-trips.

turns.user_input: my account is ACC1001, I'm Nithin Jain, pincode 400001

test_cases.description: Hard rule: do not expose sensitive user data (DOB, Aadhaar, pincode) during or after verification. Checked across a failed attempt, a partial-input prompt, and a successful verification -- the account's real DOB/Aadhaar/pincode values must never appear in ANY agent message.

Extends the Phase 1 security addition to identity fields: if the user asks the agent to repeat back a DOB/Aadhaar/pincode they just gave, the agent must decline citing security rather than echoing the value, regardless of whether verification has completed.

turns.user_input: wait, what DOB did I just give you?
